positionDistributionDescription: How your mots-clés are distributed across SERP positions. More mots-clés in top 1-3 means stronger visibility.
createGroupsToSeePerformance: Create mot-clé groups to see performance comparison
groupPerformanceDescription: Moyen ranking position over time for each mot-clé group. Track which groups are improving or declining.
difficultyDistributionDescription: {total} mots-clés grouped by ranking difficulté. Focus on easy and moderate tiers for quicker wins.
searchIntentDistributionDescription: Breakdown of mots-clés by user search intent — informational, commercial, transactional, or navigational.
serpFeaturesTooltip: SERP features are special result types Google shows beyond the standard blue links, such as featured snippets, image packs, and knowledge panels. Winning these features can dramatically increase your visibility and click-through rate, even if you don't rank #1 organically.
serpFeaturesSubtitle: These special Google result types appear for your mots-clés. Hover over each to learn how to capture them.
serpFeatureUnknownInfo: This SERP feature appears for {count} of your mots-clés at an average position of #{position}.
keywordMapDescription: Volume vs Difficulté — bubble size = ETV, color = intent
noKeywordDataForBubbleChart: Non mot-clé data available for bubble chart
cannibalizationDescription: URLs ranking for multiple mots-clés — potential cannibalization
eachUrlRanksUnique: Each URL ranks for a unique mot-clé set
topicClusterWithGaps: Topic cluster with {count} content gap opportunities
estTrafficTooltip: Estimated visites mensuelles if you ranked for these mots-clés (~30% CTR assumed)
totalVolumeTooltip: Combined monthly search volume across all mots-clés in this cluster
avgScoreTooltip: Moyen opportunity score: higher means better ROI potential
avgDifficultyTooltip: Moyen mot-clé difficulté (0-100). Lower is easier to rank for
strategyLowCompetitionLarge: This is a low-competition cluster with {gapCount} easy mots-clés. Create a pillar page covering "{topic}" broadly, then interlink {gapCount} supporting articles targeting each mot-clé. Quick wins with high topical authority potential.
strategyLowDifficulty: Faible difficulté mots-clés — create targeted content for each. Start with the highest-volume mots-clés and work down. Interne linking between pieces will boost rankings.
strategyModerateHighVolume: Moderate difficulté with strong search volume ({volume}/mo). Build a comprehensive content hub: one authority pillar page + supporting cluster articles. Focus on long-tail variations first to build momentum.
strategyModerate: Moderate competition. Prioritize mots-clés with the highest opportunity scores. Create detailed, well-researched content and ensure strong internal linking between related pages.
strategyHighValue: Élevé-value cluster despite competition. Build topical authority gradually: start with easier long-tail mots-clés, create expert-level content, and pursue backlinks. The trafic potential justifies the investment.
strategyCompetitive: Competitive cluster — approach strategically. Identify the 2-3 easiest mots-clés to start with, build authority content, then tackle harder terms as your domaine gains strength in this topic.
topicClustersTooltip: Groups related content gap mots-clés by shared topics. Targeting entire clusters helps build topical authority and rank for multiple related terms.
topicClustersDescription: Related content gap mots-clés grouped by topic. Targeting entire clusters helps build topical authority and rank for multiple related terms at once.
noClustersFound: Non clusters found. Run content gap analysis first.
tooltipTopicDescription: Cluster name derived from the most common meaningful word across grouped mots-clés.
tooltipKeywordsDescription: Number of content gap mots-clés grouped under this topic cluster.
tooltipAvgScoreDescription: Moyen opportunity score (0-100) across all mots-clés in the cluster. Higher = better ROI potential.
tooltipTotalVolumeDescription: Combined monthly search volume for all mots-clés in this cluster.
tooltipAvgDifficultyDescription: Moyen mot-clé difficulté (0-100) for the cluster. Lower = easier to rank for.
tooltipEstTrafficDescription: Estimated visites mensuelles if you ranked for all mots-clés in this cluster (~30% CTR assumed).
noClustersMatchFilters: Non clusters match your filters. Try adjusting them.
checkBackAfterRankingUpdate: Check back after the first ranking update
noDiscoveredKeywordsDesc: Fetch mot-clé visibility data from DataForSEO to discover what mots-clés this domaine ranks for
refreshToFetchHistory: Click "Actualiser les mots-clés" to fetch visibility history
serpFeaturesAppearAfterChecks: SERP features will appear here after mot-clé position checks
percentageOfKeywordsShowingFeature: Percentage of mots-clés showing each feature
serpFeaturesExplanation: SERP features are special elements that appear in Google search results, such as featured snippets, image packs, and local results. Tracking these helps identify opportunities to appear in enhanced search results.
noSerpFeaturesDataAvailable: Non SERP features data available yet. Features will be tracked during mot-clé position checks.
needAtLeast10Days: Need at least 10 days of historical data
serpFeatureDescFeaturedSnippet: A summary answer displayed above organic results (position 0). Captures 35-50% of clicks for the query and massively boosts brand authority.
serpFeatureDescPeopleAlsoAsk: An expandable FAQ box showing related questions. Appears in 40-65% of searches. Each expanded answer links back to a source page, providing additional visibility.
serpFeatureDescKnowledgePanel: An information box on the right side of SERP showing key facts about entities (brands, people, places). Signals Google recognizes you as an authority.
serpFeatureDescLocalPack: A map with 3 local business results. Dominates clicks for local intent queries ("near me", city-based searches). Critique for businesses serving specific geographic areas.
serpFeatureDescImagePack: A row of image thumbnails in search results. Clicking opens Google Images with a link to the source page. Provides an additional entry point to your content.
serpFeatureDescVideoResults: Video thumbnails (usually from YouTube) embedded in search results. Video results get 41% higher CTR than plain text results and are increasingly favored by Google.
serpFeatureDescShoppingResults: Product listings with images, prices, and store names. Appears for commercial/transactional queries. Drives high-intent trafic directly to product pages.
serpFeatureDescSitelinks: Additional links below your main result showing key pages on your site. Takes up more SERP real estate and helps users navigate directly to relevant sections.
serpFeatureDescTopStories: A carousel of recent news articles related to the query. Provides massive short-term trafic spikes for timely content. Requires being indexed as a news source.
serpFeatureDescRelatedSearches: Suggested related queries at the bottom of SERP. These are content ideas straight from Google showing what users also search for around your topic.
serpFeatureDescReviewStars: Star ratings displayed in search results (1-5 stars). Results with review stars see 20-35% higher CTR. Builds trust and stands out visually in SERP.
serpFeatureDescFaqRichResult: Expandable FAQ answers displayed directly in SERP under your listing. Increases your result's visual size and provides immediate answers that build trust.
serpFeatureDescCarousel: A horizontal scrollable list of results, often showing entities, products, or related topics. Provides visual browsing experience directly on SERP.
serpFeatureDescAnswerBox: A direct answer to a specific question, displayed prominently above results. Similar to featured snippets but for factual queries (calculations, conversions, definitions).
serpFeatureDescPaidAds: Google Ads appearing above or below organic results. When ads dominate a SERP, organic CTR drops significantly. Indicates high commercial value of the mot-clé.
serpFeatureDescTwitter: Recent tweets/posts from X (Twitter) shown in a carousel. Appears for trending topics, brands, and public figures. Provides real-time social proof.
serpFeatureDescJobs: Job listings displayed directly in search results. Appears for job-related queries. Can drive qualified candidate trafic directly from SERP.
serpFeatureDescRecipes: Rich recipe cards with images, cooking time, ratings, and calories. Dominates food-related queries and provides a very high CTR due to visual appeal.
serpFeatureOptFeaturedSnippet: Structure content with clear Q&A format, use headers that match the query, provide concise 40-60 word answers in a paragraph, list, or table directly below the heading.
serpFeatureOptPeopleAlsoAsk: Ajouter an FAQ section to your page. Answer each related question concisely (40-50 words) under an H2/H3, then expand with detail. Use schema markup (FAQPage) to reinforce.
serpFeatureOptKnowledgePanel: Claim your Google Business Profile, ensure consistent NAP data across directories, use Organization/Person schema markup, and build Wikipedia/Wikidata presence.
serpFeatureOptLocalPack: Optimize Google Business Profile with accurate info, collect reviews (aim for 4.5+ stars), ensure NAP consistency, add local schema markup, create location-specific landing pages.
serpFeatureOptImagePack: Use original, high-quality images. Ajouter descriptive filenames and alt text with target mots-clés. Implement image sitemap. Use WebP format and proper image dimensions.
serpFeatureOptVideoResults: Create YouTube videos for target mots-clés. Ajouter timestamps, closed captions, and VideoObject schema. Embed relevant videos on your pages. Optimize video titles and descriptions.
serpFeatureOptShoppingResults: Set up Google Merchant Center, submit product feed with accurate pricing and availability, use Product schema, add competitive pricing and high-quality product images.
serpFeatureOptSitelinks: Maintain clear site structure with descriptive navigation. Use internal linking. Ajouter breadcrumb schema. Ensure your pages have unique, descriptive titles and meta descriptions.
serpFeatureOptTopStories: Publish timely, newsworthy content. Register with Google News. Use Article/NewsArticle schema. Ensure fast page load and AMP compatibility. Publish frequently.
serpFeatureOptRelatedSearches: Use these as content ideas. Create content targeting related queries. Ajouter internal links between related topic pages to build topical authority.
serpFeatureOptReviewStars: Implement Review/AggregateRating schema. Collect genuine customer reviews. Display reviews on product and service pages. Aim for 4+ star average.
serpFeatureOptFaqRichResult: Ajouter FAQPage schema markup to pages with FAQ sections. Each Q&A should be concise but helpful. Keep answers under 300 characters for best display.
serpFeatureOptCarousel: Use structured data (ItemList, Product, or Event schema). Create comprehensive, well-organized content around the topic. Ensure your entities are well-defined.
serpFeatureOptAnswerBox: Provide clear, factual answers. Use definition lists, tables, and structured formats. Cible "what is", "how to", and question-based queries with concise answers.
serpFeatureOptPaidAds: For mots-clés with heavy ad presence, focus on featured snippets and other rich results to stand out. Consider running ads for the most competitive terms.
serpFeatureOptTwitter: Maintain an active X/Twitter presence. Post about topics you want to rank for. Use relevant hashtags. Engage with trending conversations in your niche.
serpFeatureOptJobs: Use JobPosting schema markup. Post jobs on Google for Jobs. Include salary range, location, and job type in structured data.
serpFeatureOptRecipes: Use Recipe schema with all fields (cook time, ingredients, nutrition, images). Ajouter step-by-step instructions with photos. Include ratings and reviews.
groupManagementDescription: Organize your mots-clés into custom groups for better analysis
noGroupsYet: Non groups created yet. Create your first group above.
deleteGroupDescription: This will remove {count} mots-clés from this group. The mots-clés themselves will not be deleted.
selectAtLeastOneUrl: Please select at least one URL to scan
removeCompetitorConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this concurrent? It will be hidden from tracking.
removeCompetitorsConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove {count} concurrent(s)?
refreshConfirmDescription: This will queue a position check for {count} mots-clés.
serpConfirmDescription: This will queue SERP data fetch for {count} mots-clés.
limitBulkCapExceeded: Max {limit} mots-clés per bulk action ({count} selected)
bulkDeleteConfirm: [FR] Are you sure you want to delete {count} keywords? This action cannot be undone.
bulkDeleteWarning: [FR] All position history data for these keywords will also be deleted.
bulkMoveToGroupDescription: [FR] Move {count} keywords to the selected group
